name: Cypress
what_it_does: JavaScript-first end-to-end and component testing framework that runs directly in the browser. Features a real-time interactive test runner, automatic waiting, time-travel debugging with screenshots, and Cypress Cloud for test recording and parallelization.
use_cases: End-to-end testing of JavaScript/TypeScript web applications
Component testing for React, Vue, Angular, and Svelte components in isolation
Visual debugging of failing tests with time-travel screenshots
Test parallelization and results recording via Cypress Cloud
API testing using cy.request() alongside UI test flows
not_for: Multi-browser testing requiring Firefox/Safari at the same fidelity as Chrome (limited support)
Non-JavaScript test codebases (Java, Python, C# teams)
best_when: A JavaScript team needs an ergonomic E2E testing solution with excellent debugging UX and component test support in the same tool.
avoid_when: You need first-class Firefox/Safari testing, multi-language SDK support, or your app relies heavily on cross-origin iframes.

auth.notes: Cypress Cloud uses a project key and record key (CYPRESS_RECORD_KEY) for CI integration. The core OSS framework requires no authentication. Record key should be treated as a secret in CI environments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: OSS framework is fully free. Cypress Cloud free tier: 500 test results/month, 1 user.
pricing.paid_tiers: Cypress Cloud Pro from $67/mo (5 users, 2,500 test results), Business from $300/mo
pricing.notes: The open-source CLI is completely free with no restrictions. Cypress Cloud adds test recording, parallelization, and analytics. Free Cloud tier is quite limited for team use.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Cypress errors shown in the interactive runner are very readable with DOM snapshots. In headless/CI mode, errors are less rich. Assertion failures include the expected vs. actual values. Error messages in CI output can be verbose and hard to parse programmatically.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Tests are idempotent when properly written. Cypress resets state between tests via beforeEach hooks. No API-level idempotency concerns since it is a local tool.
known_agent_gotchas: Cypress is JavaScript/TypeScript only - agents generating tests in other languages cannot use it
Cross-origin navigation is restricted by default (requires cy.origin() workaround in v12+)
The Electron-based GUI is unavailable in headless server environments; agents must use --headless flag
CYPRESS_RECORD_KEY must be set in environment for Cypress Cloud recording - easy to miss in ephemeral environments
cy.wait() with arbitrary milliseconds is an anti-pattern but common in agent-generated tests
Component testing requires framework-specific configuration that can be tricky to auto-generate

score_rationale: Cypress is a JavaScript-only local SDK with no REST API, no MCP server, and limited multi-language support - all factors that reduce agent-readiness. The interactive debugger is excellent for human developers but irrelevant to agents. The framework is solid for JS-heavy teams but Playwright's broader language support and more predictable headless behavior make it a stronger choice for agent-driven test generation. Cypress Cloud adds a hosted surface but lacks a well-documented REST API for programmatic orchestration.

security_score_components.security_notes: Cypress Cloud API key. Local Cypress runs require no auth. Browser-based testing — credentials typed in tests may be captured in screenshots/videos. Use Cypress env vars for secrets, never hardcode.
